text:
Once upon a time in a quaint village surrounded by ancient forests, there lived a clumsy owl named Oliver. Every morning, Oliver would fill his tiny owl sanctuary with the scent of damp moss and feathered pets he'd inexplicably raise. His favorite thing to read was an ancient book of forest lore; whenever he found a particularly good story, he'd read it to his pet moles.

One peculiar dusk, as the moon began its nightly vigil, Oliver heard a voice. It wasn't just any voice—it was lilting and sorrowful. Oliver peered across his window moss-covered shelf and saw a solitary deer grazing quietly. The deer seemed lost.

"Well now," thought Oliver, "that's nothing I haven't heard." He fluttered over with his night-flight speed and fluttered down to greet the deer.

"There's nothing there!" the deer mourned softly, as though talking to an absent friend.

"You've got a story?" Oliver hooted.

The deer nodded.
